message:
test(robustness): add edge-case tests for snapshot OOM and timer wraparound

Add deterministic tests for failure modes and boundary conditions in
distributed snapshots and timer wheel implementation.

Snapshot test — snapshot_huge_task_count_with_truncated_payload_returns_eof:
- Crafts payload claiming task_count = u32::MAX with truncated body
- Validates parser returns UnexpectedEof instead of attempting unbounded
  allocation, preventing OOM via crafted input

Timer wheel test — wheel_register_wraps_id_and_generation_without_immediate_collision:
- Sets next_id and next_generation to u64::MAX then registers two timers
- Verifies first handle gets u64::MAX, second wraps to 0
- Confirms both handles are distinct and independently cancellable
- Ensures no collision or state corruption across the 2^64 boundary,
  critical for long-running systems

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
